title: drosera longifolia var. obovata
intro: Drosera longifolia var. obovata, also known as long-leaved sundew, is a carnivorous plant species belonging to the Drosera genus. It is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of iridoid glycosides, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues and as an expectorant. The health benefits of Drosera longifolia var. obovata include relief from coughs, colds, and bronchitis, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Drosera longifolia var. obovata is commonly prepared as a tincture, tea, or capsule, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to promote overall respiratory health.
